hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Convert PNG, WebP, AVIF, and other images to JPG format. Adjustable quality with excellent compression—everything happens in your browser.

dropzone.supports: Supports PNG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, BMP • Up to 10 images

infoBanner.title: Converting to JPG (Lossy Format)
infoBanner.description: JPG uses lossy compression to create smaller file sizes. Adjust quality to balance file size and visual quality. Perfect for photos and web use.

formatSettings.title: Target Format Settings
formatSettings.selectionInfoEachImage: Each image will be converted to:
formatSettings.description: Select one or more target formats. When multiple formats are selected, each image will be converted to all selected formats and downloaded as a ZIP file. JPEG offers universal compatibility, WebP offers excellent compression, AVIF provides the best compression, and PNG preserves transparency losslessly.

qualitySettings.title: Quality Settings
qualitySettings.note: Applies to WebP, AVIF, and JPEG. Higher quality = larger files. 80% recommended. PNG is always lossless.

advancedSettings.title: Advanced Settings
advancedSettings.stripMetadataLabel: Strip metadata (EXIF, ICC) for smaller files
advancedSettings.stripMetadataDesc: Removing metadata can reduce file size but removes camera information, color profiles, and location data.

privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.p1: Your images are processed entirely in your browser. No files are uploaded to any server.
privacy.p2: All JPG conversion happens locally using JavaScript libraries. Your images never leave your device.
privacy.p3: No analytics on image content; only anonymous aggregate performance counters may be collected for optimization.
privacy.p4: Close the tab any time after processing completes. Your privacy is guaranteed.
privacy.notice: All processing local + server-side transient, no files stored.

howTo.title: How To Convert Images to JPG
howTo.step1Desc: Drag and drop your PNG, WebP, AVIF, or other images. JPEG files are excluded.
howTo.step2Desc: Adjust quality settings, then click convert to transform images to JPG format.
howTo.step3Desc: Download JPG images individually or as ZIP. Small files, great quality.
howTo.tipText: JPG is ideal for photos and web images where file size matters. Use 75-85% quality for best balance. For graphics with transparency, use PNG instead.

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.interactiveIntro: Interactive demo: See how quality affects file size in real-time

items.question: Why convert images to JPG format?
items.answer: JPG (JPEG) is the most popular image format for photos and web images. It uses lossy compression to create significantly smaller file sizes while maintaining good visual quality. This makes JPG perfect for sharing photos online, reducing website load times, and saving storage space.

items.question: What quality setting should I use?
items.answer: For most photos, 75-85% quality provides an excellent balance between file size and visual quality. Use 90-100% for print or archival purposes. For web thumbnails or previews, 60-75% works well. Below 60%, you'll start to notice visible compression artifacts.

items.question: Is my data safe when using this converter?
items.answer: Absolutely! All JPG conversion happens directly in your browser using JavaScript. Your images never leave your device or get uploaded to any server. This ensures complete privacy and security of your files.

items.question: What image formats can I convert to JPG?
items.answer: You can convert PNG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, BMP, and most other common image formats to JPG. The converter automatically handles the transformation while adjusting compression based on your quality settings.

items.question: When should I use JPG vs PNG?
items.answer: Use JPG for photographs, complex images with many colors, and when file size is important. Use PNG for graphics, logos, screenshots, images with text, or anything requiring transparency. JPG creates smaller files but doesn't support transparency.
